title: Loops should avoid having more than one break or goto statement
description: Loops should not have more than one "break" or "goto" statementAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misraconfusingWhy is this an issue?More InfoRestricting the number of exits from a loop is done in the interests of good structured programming. Onebreakorgotostatement is acceptable in a loop since this allows, for example, for dual-outcome loops or optimal coding.Noncompliant code exampleWith the default threshold of 1:for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (...) { break; // Compliant } else if (...) { break; // Non-compliant - second jump from loop } else { ... } } while (...) { if (...) { break; // Compliant } if (...) { break; // Non-compliant - second jump from loop } }Compliant solutionfor (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (...) { break; // Compliant } } while
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Loops should avoid having more than one break or goto statement. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
